summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete inference pipeline for generating SVG images from text prompts using Stable Diffusion v2, followed by a custom bitmap-to-SVG conversion and local leaderboard estimation. It implements a hierarchical feature extraction process that quantizes colors, extracts contours, and simplifies polygons to respect strict file size constraints while preserving visual fidelity. The pipeline evaluates each candidate SVG using a local approximation of the competition metric, combining a PaliGemma-2-based VQA score and a CLIP-based aesthetic score via a weighted harmonic mean. By iterating over multiple generation attempts per prompt and selecting the highest-scoring output, it provides a practical recipe for submission generation and runtime estimation. Readers should learn how to bridge diffusion models with vector graphics conversion, implement size-constrained SVG generation, and reliably estimate local leaderboard scores before submission.
competition_flow: Loads text prompts from a CSV, generates candidate bitmap images with Stable Diffusion, converts them to size-constrained SVGs, evaluates them against a local metric combining VQA and aesthetic scores, and outputs the highest-scoring SVG per prompt.
data_reading: Reads prompts from a CSV file using pd.read_csv('/kaggle/input/drawing-with-llms/train.csv'), extracting the 'description' column as text inputs.
data_processing: Resizes generated bitmaps, performs color quantization via k-means, extracts contours using OpenCV, simplifies polygon coordinates based on area/importance, compresses hex colors, and enforces a maximum byte size limit during SVG construction.
insights: Prompt engineering with specific prefixes, suffixes, and negative prompts significantly improves the alignment between generated images and the competition metric.
Hierarchical contour extraction combined with adaptive simplification effectively balances SVG detail against strict file size limits.
Local metric evaluation closely mirrors the official leaderboard, enabling reliable pre-submission validation.
